entry.title: Re: Issue During stock posting from purchase order
entry.content: If the stock type before clicking save icon shows the correct one (INSMK = X, quality inspection) and it only get to the wrong one after saving the document, a breakpoint can be set at statement CALL FUNCTION IN UPDATE TASK, to find out where the XEKPO-INSMK is updated.

entry.title: Re: Purchase order creation with a date that lies in the...
entry.content: The document date is proposed with the system date by default, but it can be manually changed before saving the document. To prevent the manual change to a future date, BAdI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST can be used to add the custom check and issue a custom error.

entry.title: Re: Error in changes purchase order
entry.content:
Transaction SM13 can be used to view the details of the update termination error. It will show the function name where the error happens and the error message details.

To find out what data is imported and causes the error, you may set a breakpoint at the statement CALL FUNCTION IN UPDATE TASK and change the PO to reproduce the error. When you stop at the breakpoint which calls the function module, you can check the import data and verify which entry / field is wrong.

entry.title: Re: Auto assignment of User responsible for each task in...
entry.content:
Dear Sridhar_Rangasamy,

thank you for the details provided. 

1.) This blog post describes a possibility (for the notification processor, but it is similar to task processor):

'Automatic Partner Determination for Quality notification'
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-members/automatic-partner-determination-for-quality-notification/ba-p/13480698

2.) Some relevant customizing to be found in this KBA too:

2465630 - Responsible and List name fields are not displayed and non-editable in quality notification

3.) Last, but not least, it worth to review following enhancements in transaction SMOD:

  • QQMA0006 - QM: Automatic Task Determination for Quality Notifications
  • QQMA0007 - QM: Default Values when Creating a Notification
  • QQMA0013 - QM: Default Values when Creating a Task
  • QQMA0019 - QM/PM/SM: Default Partner when Creating a Notification

I wish you a nice day,
​Albert
​***
​Read and follow the 'SAP Community Rules of Engagement' at
​https://www.sap.com/community/about/rules-of-engagement.html

entry.title: Re: What's the Best Approach to Move Consolidation Data A...
entry.content: For regular import, I would recommend to go through BW layer for transfer data between BW systems.

entry.title: Re: Unable to see the Change Log history For QDL2
entry.content:
Dear madhukar_goda,

thank you for the details provided.

The change log for transaction QDL2 is currently a missing function. Please see SAP Note

11 - Would you like to shape & influence future releases of SAP Products?

how to influence SAP Software Development.

I am sorry I could not provide a more convenient answer this time.

I wish you a nice day,
​Albert
​***
​Read and follow the 'SAP Community Rules of Engagement' at
​https://www.sap.com/community/about/rules-of-engagement.html

entry.title: Re: SAP Career Roadmap for Build Execution team
entry.content:
Hi   
If you already  strong foundation in SAP technologies and cloud environments through your hands-on experience with SAP landscape provisioning and operations.you might want to consider at this stage of your career on building broad expertise while exploring the areas that interest you most. There is no single "best" career path—SAP offers many opportunities across platform technologies, cloud operations, security, architecture, automation, and business solutions.

In addition to areas such as SAP BTP, Security & GRC, and SAP Cloud ALM, I would strongly encourage you to invest time in AI-related capabilities. AI is becoming a key part of the SAP ecosystem and is increasingly embedded in application development, automation, integration, analytics, and business processes through SAP BTP and Joule. Understanding how AI, automation, and business data work together will be valuable regardless of the specialization you choose.

Continuing gaining hands-on experience, follow relevant learning journeys, pursue certifications where appropriate, and stay curious about emerging technologies will be helpful as well.. A strong technical foundation combined with knowledge of cloud, automation, and AI will keep many career options open—from specialist and architect roles to future leadership positions.

For learning options, I recommend exploring the learn by product sites on  https://learning.sap.com/

  • SAP Business Technology Platform where yo can learn about SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), the platform for the Intelligent Enterprise.
  • Autonomous Enterprise to gain the expertise to lead an AI-powered digital transformation that unifies your data and scales your enterprise.
  • SAP Integration Suite to learn how to connect systems, automate business processes, and harmonize experiences across the extended enterprise.

These sites provide a structured path from fundamentals to advanced topics and can help you identify which area best matches your interests and career goal.

The SAP ecosystem offers many exciting career paths, so I encourage other community members to join the conversation and share their insights and experiences as well.

Wishing you all the best on your SAP career journey!

Best regards,
Margit Wagner

entry.title: Re: Welcome to our SAP Learning Group moderated by SAP Learning Experts
entry.content:
Hi @Poori_Ganesh

Welcome to our group and thank you to take a moment to raise your question.

As Assistance Developer Architect, you have for sure a strong foundation for moving into UX. Your experience understanding user needs, content strategy, and collaboration with product teams aligns well with many UX practices.

To get started, I'd recommend focusing on:

  • UX Fundamentals and Design Thinking
  • User Research and Usability Testing
  • Information Architecture
  • Interaction Design
  • Wireframing and Prototyping (for example, with Figma)
  • Accessibility and Inclusive Design

You may also find these SAP Learning resources helpful:

  • SAP S/4HANA UX for Business and Functional Experts
  • How to Deliver a Great User Experience with SAP S/4HANA 
  • Developing UIs with SAPUI5 

In addition, I've prepared a tailored SAP Learning search for you. Applying the following filters:

Keyword: UX
Level: Beginner
Role: Architect

This will help you discover learning journeys and courses that align with your current background while building a solid foundation in UX concepts and practices.

Best of luck on your UX journey.

Feel free to reach out and ask your questions please post questions in our  Q&A area. This makes it easier  to follow-up:

  • That way other learners can benefit from the answer
  • If you ask the question there other learners can search for it in the future if they run into the same issue and there we have more features available for responses

Kind regads
Margit

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 Available Now
entry.content: It is more than frustrating. I have been trying to install the ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 for over 3 months to become a SAP administrator, but I have given up because I am immensely annoyed by your nonsensical messages.

entry.title: WBS Validation Table PRTE Not Available in Boolean Class Despite VWTYGB01 and RGUGBR00 – Error GB082
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are unable to use the WBS Basic Start Date (PRTE-PSTRT) and Basic Finish Date (PRTE-PENDE) fields in our PS Validation (OPSI). This is blocking us from enforcing a mandatory date validation for Level 2 WBS elements under company codes S135 and S100, which is a key data quality control requirement for our Investment project type.


Validation Configuration:
Validation Name: ZPS01 — Scania WBS Validation
Step 008 — Bsic strt & end dates are mandatory

Prerequisite:
PRPS-PRART = 'IV' AND PRPS-STUFE = '2' AND PRPS-PBUKR = 'S135' AND PRPS-PBUKR = 'S100'

Check:
PRTE-PSTRT <> ' ' AND PRTE-PENDE <> ' '

Message: ZFI_M 106 — Error (Invalid logical system) (This message is just for the testing purpose).

Steps Performed to Make PRTE Available:
By default, the WBS date fields PRTE-PSTRT (Basic Start Date) and PRTE-PENDE (Basic Finish Date) are not available in the Validation & Substitution Boolean class structure pool for PS WBS Elements. To make them available, the following two steps were pe

entry.title: Parloa + SAP Service Cloud V2: Building a Voice AI Agent for Automated Service Request Management
entry.content:
In this blog, we explore how Parloa AI Agent Management Platform can be integrated with SAP Service Cloud Version 2 to automate a common customer service scenario: Service Request Status Inquiry and Update.

By combining Parloa's conversational Voice AI capabilities with SAP Service Cloud V2's service management foundation, organizations can deliver a seamless voice-first customer experience.

https://www.parloa.com/sap/  

Business Scenario

Imagine a manufacturing company that receives hundreds of customer calls every day asking questions such as:

  • What is the status of my service request?
  • When will my technician arrive?
  • Has my replacement product been shipped?
  • Can I update my contact number?

These calls are repetitive and consume a significant portion of support agent capacity.

Instead of waiting for an agent, customers can interact with an AI-powered voice assistant that:

  1. Understands customer requests.
  2. Authenticates the caller.
  3. Retrieves service request information from SAP Service Cloud V2.
  4. Provides status updates in natural language.
  5. Updates customer information when required.
  6. Escalates to a human agent when necessary.
+---------------------+
| Customer Phone Call |
+----------+----------+
           |
           v
+---------------------+
|      Parloa AI      |
| Voice Conversation  |
+----------+----------+
           |
           | REST APIs
           v
+---------------------+
| CTI / Middleware  |
+----------+----------+
           |
           |
           v
+---------------------+
| SAP Service Cloud V2|
| Service Requests    |
| Customers           |
| Cases               |
+----------+----------+
           |
           v
+---------------------+
| Service Agents      |
+---------------------+

Why Parloa?

Parloa provides enterprise-grade AI voice agents that can:

  • Understand natural language conversations
  • Handle multi-turn dialogues
  • Support omnichannel engagement
  • Integrate with backend business applications
  • Transfer conversations to human agents
  • Provide real-time conversation orchestration

For enterprises already running SAP Service Cloud V2, Parloa becomes the conversational front-end while SAP remains the system of record.

SAP Service Cloud V2 as the Service Backbone

SAP Service Cloud V2 offers:

  • Case Management
  • Service Requests
  • Installed Base and Registered Products
  • Customer Master Data
  • Knowledge Base
  • Service Quotes
  • Service Analytics

Parloa x SAP One Pager

 

Future Enhancements

Once the basic integration is established, organizations can extend the Voice AI agent to:

  • Create new service tickets
  • Schedule technician visits
  • Identify installed products
  • Provide warranty information
  • Trigger field service activities
  • Recommend knowledge base articles
  • Perform sentiment analysis
  • Integrate with Joule and SAP Business AI services

More details will updated soon

entry.title: Code Connect 2026: Final Info Before We Kick Off!
entry.content:
Code Connect 2026 is just around the corner! From July 13–16, 2026, the SAP developer community will gather in St. Leon-Rot, Germany – onsite and online.

Here’s everything you need to know before we get started.

 

 

We Are Fully Booked!

All onsite tickets are fully booked - thank you for the amazing interest!

If you couldn’t secure a ticket:

  • Join the waiting lists on Code Connect 2026 in case seats become available.
  • Follow selected sessions via live stream or Microsoft Teams link.

Even if you're not onsite, you can still be part of Code Connect. Selected sessions will be streamed live, and recordings of some sessions will be available afterwards. Check back soon for details.

 

 

 

UI5con (July 14)

  • Main stage sessions will be live streamed on YouTube.
  • Check the agenda to see which sessions are streamed.

 

 

re>≡CAP (July 15)

  • Main stage sessions will be broadcasted.
  • Some sidetracks (W1/W2) can be attended via Microsoft Teams link.
  • Check the agenda to see which sessions are broadcasted or available via Microsoft Teams link.

 

 

HANA Tech Con (July 16)

  • The links will be announced on the event day.

 

The Agendas Are Live

The full program is already available - time to plan your schedule!

Check-In opens at 8.00 AM every day. Please bring the QR code with you that you can find in your ticket.

July 13 – Code Jams & Community Meetup

  • Hands-on Code Jams:
    • OpenUI5
    • CAP
    • AI Agents
  • Informal pre-event meetup (no registration needed):
    Ihle Besen, Höfe am Sträßel 3, 69231 Rauenberg
    5:00 PM CEST

July 14–16 – Main Conference Days

Explore all agendas:

  • July 14: UI5con
  • July 15: re>≡CAP
  • July 16: HANA Tech Con

On July 14 also the HANA AI CodeJam takes place.

 

Location

Code Connect takes place at:

SAP-Allee 27
68789 St. Leon-Rot, Germany

Find detailed information on directions, parking, and public transport here.

 

Final Tips

  • Bookmark your favorite sessions in advance
  • Arrive early for popular sessions (rooms fill quickly!)
  • Bring your laptop for Code Jams and hands-on sessions/ workshops
  • Don’t miss the networking moments - some of the best conversations happen in between sessions
  • Follow the agendas closely for last-minute updates

 

See You Soon!

We’re looking forward to an inspiring week of learning, sharing, and connecting.

See you at Code Connect 2026!

I understood that SAP released GitHub Integration for Integration suite. I am trying to see the GitHub related options in my tenants but cant find. 

I have admin and developer role in all tenants but I am not able to see it. 

Do we have any prerequisites to enable GitHub Inytegration?

entry.content: Please consider me , i am interested

entry.title: Purchase Order Conditions Values are copied whe i create a new order for the same Supplier and Mater
entry.content: Hello, i have many Z* Conditions in Purchase Order (ZA10,ZA20 ecc) when i create the order and put values into them all works but if subsequently i create a new order for the same supplier and the same material the conditions are automatically filled with the values put in the previous order, there is a way to bypass this thing  and have the conditions empty?

entry.title: Matchning incoming payment automatically without leading zero
entry.content:
Hi, 

We are trying to create rules so incoming payments can be matched automatically in the Bank statement. 

The problem is that in the Customer Line items we have a field that starts with zero, and the customer pays without the zeros. 
Example 000000672026REPP but the customer send us this reference CINV 672026REPP. 

I have read and found that we should use Interpretation Algorithms 026 "Search for reference document number with leading zero, if <10". I have connected this algorithm to external transaction that I have added to on the bank statement row. 

But I don´t get it work, because on the rule I can´t choose this algorithm. How can I solve this?

I 

 

 

 

/Olivia

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 Build Code-based AI Agents on SAP Business AI Platform (Bengaluru, India)
entry.content: Please consider me , i am interested

entry.title: Re: New Employee Business Partner Data Model in SAP S/4HANA 2020 On-Premise
entry.content:
Hi,
Reports /SHCM/RH_SYNC_BUPA_EMPL_SINGLE  and /SHCM/RH_SYNC_BUPA_FROM_EMPL must run without errors and must create both employee-BP and employment-BP. 
Then run report /SHCM/R_EMPL_HANDLE_BPROLES (TR /SHCM/BPROLE_SYNC) respective in background /SHCM/R_EMPL_HDLE_BPRLES_DELTA (TR /SHCM/BPROLE_SYNCJOB). Please check that both bank data and address data must exist at employee for vendor role creation.

Please also note this SAP note:
2578294 - FAQ - Employee to Business Partner synchronization in S/4HANA OnPremise

BR
Dieter

entry.title: Re: Candidate Rehire Check from SmartRecruiters when integrating with SuccessFactors
entry.content: @DharaniR As of today 6 July 2026 you can only send external hires from SR to ONB or EC.  Internal hire flow is coming later this year.

entry.title: Re: Error in changes purchase order
entry.content:
Could you please confirm the exact SAP version and context (e.g., recent development, ongoing upgrade) where this issue occurred?

Additionally:

  1. Error Details: What specific error or FM call did you observe in transaction SM13 or sometimes ST22?
  2. OSS Notes: Have you reviewed relevant SAP notes, such as KBA 3670584 - When creating purchase order, 'update was cancelled' is raised - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA or 1961083 - Express document: "Update termination" received when using ME22N / ME22 - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA.

entry.title: Re: Recruit-to-Hire with multiple payComponents
entry.content: We’re facing a similar situation: one country requires multiple pay components to be mapped, while another needs only a single component. Unfortunately, the amount field is mandatory, and this is causing the BPE to fail. Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thank you.

thank you for the details provided. 

What do you mean exactly by "non-stock item"? Please explain your scenario and requirement with some example.

Thank you and I wish you a nice day,
​Albert

​https://www.sap.com/community/about/rules-of-engagement.html

entry.title: Re: When was the last time that you upgraded your supply chain planning system?
entry.content:
Great Blog on the key capabilities of SAP IBP!  

And if you would like to enhance your planning process even more by adding the supplier dimension, have a look into SAP Business Network Planning Collaboration . Forecasts and demand plans from IBP are then shared directly with trading partners. Supplier commitments, inventory levels, and deviations flow back in real time.

entry.title: Re: A Comprehensive Guide to Transition from SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition to advanced editi
entry.content:
Hello,

We have exported Site settings from Build Workzone Standard edition and wanted to import it Build Workzone Advanced edition but.......there is no option to import anything under Site Directory in Advanced edition:

However, there is an option to import Content under Content Manager:

How should we proceed here? 

Isn't there an option to migrate Site settings (export ->import) on Site level at all? Do we have to do this only on Content Manager level?

entry.title: Re: Cannot post document: tax base in local currency is z...
entry.content:
Could you please confirm which SAP system version you are referring to in your request? Specifically, we need to know whether it is:

  • SAP ECC (ERP Central Component)
  • SAP S/4HANA (on-premise)
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud (public)
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud (private edition)

(Please use some relevant SAP managed tag)

Could you also specify:

  • Document Type: Is this related to an inbound vendor invoice, outbound customer invoice, or another document type?
  • Processing Tool: Which tool is being used to handle this document (e.g., transaction code, Fiori app, or interface)?

Did you already look for some KBA 

  • Look for message FF759

entry.title: Re: Sending Emails to Personal Email id / Sending multipl...
entry.content:
Hi @subhashinipt ,

Emails to employees are always sent to their Business Email. Only option I see: the users have to add their Private Email to email type business. But I would never recommend this or sending Business Emails from EC to a private Email address.

And only one email per CC Role. If you want to send different email email templates you have to add the same external email multiple times to the workflow with different email templates.

 

BR,

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Changing the unit of measure during RF packing in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi @Shubh07 ,

I agree with you. If the pointer is at HU field, it allows "SF2". But unfortunately, in the RF Gun, it comes to the quantity field verification automatically. We will ask them to go to HU field as a workaround to allow "SF2" to open up UOM.

Thanks,

Sreeram G

entry.title: Re: Can we block position Update if done with the wrong E...
entry.content:
Hi @pboulicaut ,

SAP is providing the solution you have already found.

Why would you need to adjust the position effective date after an employee is already assigned to that position?

 

BR,

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Purchase order creation with a date that lies in the...
entry.content:
Could you please confirm which SAP system version you are referring to in your request? Specifically, we need to know whether it is:

  • SAP S/4HANA (on-premise)
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud (public)
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud (private edition)

Depending on version, there are some available BAdIs such as ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST or BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO for example.

entry.title: Is Your Payment Stack Ready for the Holiday Rush?
entry.content:
What is the Open Payment Framework (OPF)?

OPF is a low-code payment integration platform built directly into SAP Commerce Cloud. It addresses a long-standing challenge for SAP merchants: traditionally, integrating payment service providers (PSPs) required custom development, code extensions, and ongoing maintenance tied to Commerce releases.

OPF removes that dependency. Merchants and partners can configure PSP integrations through a dedicated workbench, no custom code to write, deploy, or maintain. Payment integration becomes a configuration task rather than a development project.

OPF Key Capabilities

  • No additional cost: OPF is available to all SAP Commerce Cloud customers as part of their existing contract.
  • Faster time to value: PSP integrations can be configured and deployed significantly faster than traditional custom development, reducing go-live timelines and dependency on development resources.
  • Lower total cost of ownership: no custom code means no maintenance burden when SAP Commerce Cloud is updated.
  • Flexible payment flows: OPF supports authorization, capture, refund, cancellation, and notifications, as well as front-end patterns including iFrame, hosted pages, and hosted fields.
  • Payment partner ecosystem, ready to activate: integrations with PayPal and Checkout.com partnership are available without writing a line of code. Also, OPF supports APMs, Apple Pay, Google Pay.
  • Merchant visibility and control: built-in logging, transaction visibility, and manual status overrides for customer service agents.
  • B2B ready: OPF supports specific flows such as manual and account-based payments. Merchants running B2B storefronts on SAP Commerce Cloud can enforce flexible organization controls, and establish governance capabilities that reduce operational risk, staying operationally lean.

Want to learn more? Join our dedicated Webcast:

Webcast on  July 9, 2026

Getting Holiday-Ready with SAP Commerce Cloud Payments: Unlocking Peak Season Success with Open Payment Framework and Leading Payment Partners

Thursday, 9 July 2026  |  4:00 PM CEST  |  1hour 15 minutes  |  Online – Live

The session covers SAP Commerce Cloud's payment strategy and a hands-on look at OPF  how it enables agility and provider flexibility at scale. You will also hear directly from two of SAP's newest OPF partners, PayPal and Checkout.com:

  • insights on authorization rate optimization and building a resilient payment setup for high-traffic periods.
  • a concrete example of OPF's value in practice: merchants on SAP Commerce Cloud can activate a payment method without any custom development.

Speakers:
Fagner Santos: SAP Lead Product Manager
Featuring expert insights from industry leaders at PayPal and Checkout.com 

Register here

Resources

    • Training- OPF Microlearning Videos
    • Onboarding guide
    • Help center documentation
    • Demo Video - SAP Commerce Cloud, open payment framework Overview - SAP Media Share
    • SAP Commerce Cloud, Open Payment Framework | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Parameters of Multi action not taking updated story filters
entry.content:
2 story filter on

1-Cost object property (L2)

2-Cost object filter with hierarchy. 

Cascading effect is ON.

I have selected one L2 and accordingly cost object filter getting changed.

I have a multi action which is using Cost object(story filter) as parameter. The issue is it is taking the topmost level of the hierarchy and displaying all L2s under it even though in story filter only 1 L2 is visible.

Is this is a bug where story filters as parameters are not taking updated results.

Please find here the deck and recording: https://community.sap.com/t5/max-success-plan-blog-posts/innovation-workshop-accelerate-consulting-productivity-with-sap-joule-4/ba-p/14428026#M57

Ann-Christin Schechter

entry.title: Re: Alumni: mass load to convert former employees as Alumni
entry.content: PLease post this in the Blog Section and not as a question, to be able to get the support you deserve.

entry.title: Advance Holiday Pay (AHP) UK
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently going through a UK SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) implementation and are looking for some guidance from other customers.

One of our payroll requirements is to pay holiday pay in advance for weekly paid employees. For example, where an employee is due to be paid for a week's holiday, the holiday pay would be paid in the payroll period before the holiday is taken.

Historically, we understood that SAP UK Payroll supported Advance Holiday Pay (AHP) functionality, but during our ECP implementation there is some uncertainty around whether this functionality is still available and supported as standard.

We're keen to hear from other customers who have addressed a similar requirement:

  • Are you using standard Advance Holiday Pay (AHP) functionality in ECP UK?
  • How are you managing the requirement to pay weekly employees in advance of a holiday period?
  • Was standard SAP functionality sufficient, or was custom development required?
  • Have you encountered any challenges during implementation or migration from SAP ECC Payroll to ECP?
  • Has SAP provided any guidance on the recommended approach for this scenario?

We're trying to understand whether this is a gap in our current design/configuration or whether other organisations have had similar experiences.

Any advice, lessons learned, or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Many thanks,

Stuart

entry.title: Re: 4-Eyes by Configuration: Multi-Processor Workflow Approvals in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content: @Egbert_de_Jong thx for the hint on Business Object Type. My answer depends on the application delivered a flexible workflow scenario for these business objects (in that case a rework task could be added to the scenario if not there). WO is work orders? SE means shopping card? Is there "Manage workflows for SE/WO" and a scenario implemented? (also depends on your S/4 version.)

entry.title: Re: OData v2 upsert on custom MDF Object creates record b...
entry.content:
Hi @YABAPER ,

 

have you already checked this KBA:

Title: 2396714 - Workflow not Triggered via API or Integration Center
Link: https://me.sap.com/notes/2396714

entry.title: Re: Autofill not populating in SAP SRM 7.1 4 SP022 after...
entry.content:
Hi Alisa,

This looks less like an SRM customizing issue and more like a browser compatibility/regression issue introduced with the Chromium 149 update. SAP has a relevant KBA: 3763555 – WEBGUI: History not working after Chrome / Edge 149 update.

regards

Andrea

entry.title: Join Us Tomorrow: SAP BTP ABAP Environment Customer & Partner Roundtable – July 2026
entry.content:
Dear customers and partners, 

Join us tomorrow for the next session of our SAP BTP ABAP Environment Customer & Partner Roundtable, taking place on July 07, 2026, from 10:00–11:00 CET.

Our roundtable is a monthly virtual meeting where we share the latest updates from the product team and showcase selected topics from our experts. It is a great opportunity to stay up to date with the latest capabilities of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment, ask questions, and engage with the community.

Join the session

📅 Date: July 07, 2026
⏰ Time: 10:00–11:00 CET

Agenda

🔹 Product Updates – Frank Jentsch

🔹 New ABAP Language Features - Cedric Becker, Rolf Hammer
 

The roundtable is a monthly series taking place on the first Tuesday of every month (10:00–11:00 CET).

👉 Download the .ics calendar file for the full 2026 series and import it directly into your calendar.
Each calendar entry already includes the Microsoft Teams meeting link.

Microsoft Teams meeting link (valid for the entire 2026 series): Microsoft Teams meeting link 

All agendas, slides, recordings, and updates from past and current sessions are published centrally via the
SAP Community – SAP BTP ABAP Environment Customer & Partner Roundtable.

We’re looking forward to seeing you there!

SAP BTP ABAP Environment Team

entry.title: Re: Joule A2A: Propagate the User Identity from Joule into S/4HANA
entry.content: @felixbartler Gem of a blog... Config + Testing!!! Thanks for it.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 Available Now
entry.content: Before frustration gets the best of you, i'd advise checking out SAP Cloud Appliance Library in the meanwhile https://cal.sap.com/ . As far as i know, you only need a registered account to bring up these instances.

entry.title: Re: 2 (mandatory) Email Adresses for new hires/ in people...
entry.content:
Hi @christian_braun ,

why do you want to have an automatism that you as an employee will be forced to maintain a business email?

Normally there is an integration between SuccessFactors and any kind of AD system which is replicating the business email to EC as soon as the employee has been maintained as new hire in EC.

You as an employee will either add your private email during an Onboarding process or will be able to store is optionally as of your first working day directly in EC.

entry.title: Budget calculation based on Lookup Table in EC-integrated Compensation Template
entry.content:
Hello SAP SuccessFactors experts,

We are currently using a non-Employee Central integrated Compensation template.

Our budget is calculated based on a Lookup Table, which allows us to assign different budget percentages depending on employee-specific criteria.

Next year, we plan to migrate to an Employee Central integrated Compensation template.

I found documentation stating that budget calculations and budget assignments are supported in EC-integrated templates, but I could not find clear information regarding the use of Lookup Tables as the basis for budget calculation.

My questions are:

  1. Is it possible to continue using a Lookup Table for budget determination in an EC-integrated Compensation template?
  2. If yes, are there any limitations compared to a non-EC integrated template?

Any experiences, best practices, or references to SAP documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

Best regards,
Joe

entry.title: Re: SF EC MDF UI Hacks: When RBP Isn’t Enough to Hide Fie...
entry.content:
Hi @JoshDev ,

 

nice solution. But please post it in the blog section so that all members will find it in the right place.

 

Thank you.

We have the Error "The Create button is disabled in the Product Specific Configuration phase. Please use the Edit button in the Scope section to make necessary changes and access the app"

 

 
Our workplace go to "Product-Specific Configuration Phanse", so we can not create new SO tye ?

entry.title: Re: GitHub Integration is not available for tenants

Hi @JayneZ ,

are you still facing this issue after 1H2026 release?

If so, please add screenshot of the Role Assignments when coming from RBP troubleshooting and when opening the Role directly.

Thank you.

BR,
Sandra

please reach out to SAP education of SAP Partners who provide SuccessFactors Learning, if the learnings available via the SAP Community are not sufficient.

entry.title: Re: Can SAP Joule Skills Be Triggered by External Events (e.g., Incoming Email)?
entry.content: I believe this could certainly be achieved while you use the API call during your skill designing. You will also have to look for the configuration of Microsoft server too for this API call with your Build Studio.

entry.title: Re: Bank statement upload - change posting rule with search pattern function
entry.content: There is no such functionality available in SAP

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 Available Now
entry.content:
Hallo @Ruben01 ,

unfortunately that is not true, otherwise we'd surely all switch to that installation. If you look into the details of the images, you will find a cost estimator which calculates the expected costs depending on the hyperscaler. With CAL you only get the images, not the infrastructure....

Best regards,

Alexander

entry.title: Re: while enabling the Best Practice Configurations emplo...
entry.content:
@HariKrishna721 Please try if an ODataRefresh, MDF Refresh and RBP Refresh will solve this.

And refresh your BCUI.

If something in the configuration might be missing, you need to open a support ticket.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Happy to join this community.

entry.title: Re: I want to create a business rule to trigger alert not...
entry.content:
@AdityaBorkar Please attach a screenshot of your Alert Rule.

It might not be possible to check if the user has direct reports, because the assignment is from bottom to top and not vice versa.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud - Configure Value Mapping : how to fetch in ABAP Cloud ?
entry.content: Hi, I also facing issue in reading the value mapping, have you found the solution for this ? Thank you

entry.title: MTO, quantity in SO change. How should I deal with Purchase order ?
entry.content:
My customer model is MTO. They have case as bellow

1. They already input SO into SAP, MRP run , generated Planned order and Purchase requistion

2. Purchasing department converted PR into PO already

3. Thier customer changed order quantity in SO.

4. MRP runs agian and change the requirement. However, the PR is already convered into PO so MRP can not change quantity in PO

5. Thier BOM include 100 components. Not all supplier allow to change order quantity in thier PO

In this case, how system can help the user to know which material they should change how many quantity in PO? I want it to be an report, other than system automatically changes the quantity in PO because not all supplier allow that.

Please help to suggest a solution

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: How to rename an OData V4 service?
entry.content:
You are right, I should have put "renaming" in double quotes.


Option 2 + 3 offer you the option to have a second service URL instead of having to use the original service URL but now with a changed behavior.

Which option to choose would depend on your use case. 

I you use option 1 you have to make sure to change your deployed app simultaneously because otherwise it would stop to work.

Option 3 is not "unrelated". It shows how you extend or develop further your existing service and publish the new service not only with a new URL but as a new service and it would hence require you to assign appropriate authorizations to your end users as well.

entry.title: Re: How to limit/hide content from an ADMIN permission ro...
entry.content:
Hi, 

you need to exclude the own peers from the target people pool. And if you need to run reports for ALL including peers, you might slit that in 2 roles with different target populations.

entry.title: Data Sphere Package assignment issue for the objects
entry.content:
hi Team,

In Data sphere development, though the objects are under package, when we open that particular object package assignment showing as "None". For example, data sphere view Y2_VG_DI_CROS_PROFCNTR_001 is available under package PROFITCENTER_MD. But when we open the object package showing as "None". Because of this issue, when the object is already moved to production, if there are any changes to the objects in DEV it is difficult to find the actual package where the object is already available.

Please check the issue.

 

Thanks

Ramya

entry.title: Course Spotlight: Introducing the New SAP IBP Harmonized Planning Area Course (IBP3910)
entry.content:
Supply chain planning has never been more complex — and the tools planners use, need to match that complexity with clarity, not add to it. That's exactly what the Harmonized Planning Area (HPA) in SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) is designed to do. And now, there's a dedicated course to help you unlock its full potential. 

 What is the Harmonized Planning Area? 

The Harmonized Planning Area is a unified planning foundation within SAP IBP that brings together demand, supply, inventory, and response planning into a single, consistent data model. Rather than maintaining separate planning areas for each process — each with its own master data, key figures, and configurations — HPA gives planners and architects one coherent structure to work from. 

This is a significant evolution. It eliminates the data silos that have historically forced teams to reconcile key figures across disconnected planning views. When everyone plans from the same area, the numbers tell one consistent story. 

 Why Does This Matter? 

HPA unlocks a new level of planning maturity for IBP customers. By standardizing on a harmonized model, organizations can now: 

  • Reduce configuration effort — a single planning area consolidates master data, key figures, and operator setups, freeing teams to focus on planning decisions rather than maintenance overhead.
  • Accelerate S&OP alignment — demand and supply teams work from one unified data model, eliminating reconciliation friction and enabling faster, more confident consensus planning. 
  • Shorten time-to-value — new planning scenarios and use cases can be onboarded within the existing HPA structure, dramatically reducing implementation timelines. 
  • Ensure consistent data integration — RTI provides a single pipeline for TSP and OBP master data, guaranteeing consistent master data between ERP and end-to-end planning in IBP.

Together, these capabilities give cross-functional teams a shared language for planning decisions and a foundation that scales with the business — without the complexity that came with managing multiple isolated planning areas. 

 What IBP3910 Teaches — Unit by Unit 

Structured as a 2-day instructor-led training with 10 hands-on exercises, the course takes participants from foundational architecture concepts all the way through live planning runs in I_SAPIBP2. Here is what each unit covers: 

 

                                      

This isn't just conceptual. The course is designed with real- world scenarios so you leave ready to apply what you've learned on your next engagement. 

 The Business Case for Your Customers 

 For customers running SAP IBP, the value proposition is compelling: 

  • Faster planning cycles — a unified model means fewer handoffs and less time spent aligning numbers, especially between operational and tactical supply planning 
  • Lower total cost of ownership — one planning area to configure, maintain, and upgrade 
  • Greater agility — new business requirements can be modeled without rebuilding the planning foundation 
  • Better decisions — planners work from a single version of the truth, improving confidence in the numbers driving executive decisions.  

In a world where supply chains need to respond to disruptions in hours, not days, this kind of structural agility is a competitive differentiator. 

Who Is This Course For? 

IBP3910 is designed for professionals directly involved in planning, implementing, or governing SAP IBP environments: 

  • Application Consultants: Implementing or upgrading SAP IBP for clients — get ahead of HPA architecture before you hit the project. 
  • Project Stakeholders: Leading supply chain transformation programs — understand what the HPA changes and what it unlocks for your roadmap. 
  • Planning Power Users: Working daily in SAP IBP — transition confidently to I_SAPIBP2 and take advantage of unified planning workflows. 

The link to the course : 

IBP3910 - Discovering SAP IBP Harmonized Planning Area 

Happy Learning!

entry.title: Re: Workforce Replication from Employee Central to SAP Ma...
entry.content:
Hi @Dominik_Bender ,

have you checked this Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-integration-suite-integration-with-sap-master-data-integration-mdi/ba-p/13525349

BR,

Sandra

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 Available Now
entry.content:
Hi Alexander,

That is correct, you need to supply AWS or Azure creds for it to to work. the infra part will be hosted in one of these cloud providers if i'm not mistaken. I used AWS for this to experiment and learn about basis admin things. my overall monthly cost was around 200 USD. Also i'm not sure if you get developer license there too (i'd like to think so) but for learning basis administration and experimenting with some modules and such it is perfectly fine.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Enroll in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Centr...
entry.content:
Hi @Santhoshsk15 ,

please reach out to the Support of the Training. This section is for product related questions.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: to enable the mass upload functionality embedded in t...
entry.content:
Hello Praveen, 

You may check these 2 SAP Help documents for the KP26 Excel upload functionality and configuration steps:

  • Excel Planning In Cost Center Accounting | SAP Help Portal
  • Planning Offline and Uploading into the SAP System | SAP Help Portal

Best regards,
Nghia

entry.title: Can we use a field in custom MDF and setup a business rule to trigger onboarding custom email
entry.content:
We have a custom MDF UI setup with a field (yes/no). This custom MDF UI is associated in Additional Data Collection step in Onboarding.

We have an custom email notification setup that triggers after new joiner completes onboarding process. The condition for this email is: The email should trigger to Only new hires who selected 'yes' value for the field in the custom MDF UI in ADC step.

When we create a business rule of scenario type: 'Filter Email Template' and attached the business rule in email template, we see email is triggered when new hires select either 'yes' or 'no' as the value in the custom MDF UI in ADC step.

 

@Gopinath_Murugaiyan any recommendations ?

Any suggestions here?

entry.title: Re: Candidate Rehire Check from SmartRecruiters when integrating with SuccessFactors
entry.content: @ChrisPaine - you don't have this feature in SF RCM, the system only checks for possible rehires when you initiate onboarding?  Then it will look at the 'Ok to rehire' flag on the inactive record.

entry.title: Re: Renew Certificates in Cloud Connector (Manual & Upload)
entry.content: good document. However this process is automated since version 2.18.0 of CC.

entry.title: Access Configure Value Mapping Entries from ABAP Cloud in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi experts,

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, we are using the Configure Value Mapping app and have maintained mappings such as:

 

Namespace : /EDOMY
Value Mapping Name : MY_MATERIAL_CLASSIF

 

 

From an ABAP Cloud/RAP application, I would like to retrieve these configured value mappings programmatically.

I have checked for released CDS views and APIs but could not find any obvious option.

Is there a standard way to access Configure Value Mapping data from ABAP Cloud?

Specifically:

  • Is there any released CDS view available?
  • Is there a released OData API or service?
  • Is there a released ABAP class/API for reading value mappings?
  • Or are these mappings only intended for internal framework consumption and not accessible from custom ABAP Cloud code?

My use case is to retrieve the classification code mapping maintained in MY_MATERIAL_CLASSIF during a RAP action implementation.

 

I found similar post but I dont really see the solution for this issue : Solved: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud - Configure Value Mapping... - SAP Community

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: 勘定 827130減価償却費 に CO 対象への割当が必要です
entry.content:
Dear Sir,

Thank you for reaching out.

Please refer to the following KBA for guidance on this issue: https://me.sap.com/notes/2217322

Should you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact us.

Best regards,
Nghia

entry.title: WHT amount is not appearing for few of the invoices in FBL1N report
entry.content: WHT has been calculated for both highlighted line items. However, the report displays the WHT for only one of the line items. Could you please check why it is not appearing for the other line items.(The vendor code is same for all the line items).

entry.title: Bank Communication Capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Portugal
entry.content:
Hi,

Is it possible to implement the following bank communication services in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Exchange Rates Reception
SEPA Credit Transfer (SEPA CT)
MT101 Payment Orders
Portuguese Tax Payments (DUC), Social Security Payments, and Multibank References
Confirming Payment Orders
SEPA Direct Debit (SEPA DD)

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: GitHub Integration is not available for tenants
entry.content: Thanks you @Yogananda

entry.title: Re: Which document number or what is important to jump to...
entry.content:
Dear Isabella,

You may try using field REFBN (Reference Document Number) instead of BELNR (CO Document Number) in the layout configuration — this should navigate you to the original FI document instead of the CO document.

Best regards,
Nghia

entry.title: Re: Debugging SAP Smartforms : Smart tip to debug SAP smartforms
entry.content:
I guess you used SFTRACE with default trace level 5.

Instead, use the trace level 80.

You'll get something like that:

-------------------------------------------------------
|00|trace begin level 80                              |
|00|trace version 046C01                              |
|00|time stamp 20161215151746                         |
|00|locale date 15.12.2016                            |
|00|locale time 16:17:46                              |
|00|system information                                |
|00|  sap version 740                                 |
|00|  operating system AIX                            |
|00|  database system ORACLE                          |
|00|  database name ZZ1                               |
|00|  user name SR0D12FN                              |
|15|open                                              |
|15|  device PRINTER                                  |
|15|  output device LOCL                              |
|15|  device type SAPWIN                              |
|15|  archive mode print only                         |
|15|generating output table definition                |
|15|generating output table definition                |
|15|generating output table definition                |
|15|generating output table definition                |
|15|generating output table definition                |
|15|generating output table definition                |
|15|generating output table definition                |
|15|generating output table definition                |
|15|generating output table definition                |
|15|generating output table definition                |
|15|process document                                  |
|15|structure form header                             |
|15|  form name ZSHEETLET_GESTION                     |
|15|  language EN                                     |
|15|  version 00001                                   |
|15|  variant                                         |
|15|  start page FEUILLET                             |
|15|  style SYSTEM                                    |
|15|  dynamic start page not set                      |
|15|structure document description                    |
|15|  page SHEETLET                                   |
|15|  next page SHEETLET                              |
|15|    window LOGO type graphic                      |
|15|    callprog /1BCDWB/SAPLSF00000005               |
|15|    callform %WI1                                 |
|15|    window TITLE_LOWERANCE type text              |
|15|    callprog /1BCDWB/SAPLSF00000005               |
|15|    callform %WI2                                 |
|15|    window LOWERANCE type text                    |
|15|    callprog /1BCDWB/SAPLSF00000005               |
|15|    callform %WI3                                 |
|15|    window TITLE type text                        |
|15|    callprog /1BCDWB/SAPLSF00000005               |
|15|    callform %WI4                                 |
|15|generating form header and document structure     |
|05|! A SSFCOMPOSER 050                               |
|05|  MSGV1: ZCOURIER                                 |
|05|trace output closed due to error                  |
-------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Re: Change in description of BOMs
entry.content: Dear Blagovesta, the issue appears to belong to an ERP feature (S4 HANA Cloud public cloud perhaps). Please change the SAP-Managed tag to "SD (Sales and Distribution)"? Thank you and best regards, Oliver

entry.title: Re: Issue with Accessing the Fiori App (F5373)
entry.content:
Hello Yuji, 

I believe that you would need to add ICF node to ZFCO MNG COST ELEMENT GROUP. 

I could see that it is empty in the bottom left corner. 

Please let me know if this work. 

Best regards,
Nghia

entry.title: Re: 504: GATEWAY TIMEOUT error in Cloud Foundry Environment
entry.content: Dear elamparithi666, your question appears to concern Cloud Foundry runtime. I recommend changing the SAP-Managed tag to "SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment". Kind regards, Oliver

entry.title: Re: Can the requester specify/select an ad-hoc approver w...
entry.content:
Dear @byd,

Question 1.

No. In the standard Manage Workflows for Supplier Invoices app the approver is determined by the workflow configuration. SAP documents that supplier invoice workflows can be configured with one-step or multi-step approval and that approvers can be assigned to each step. SAP also documents approver determination options such as cost center responsible, specific users, teams and custom agent determination. However, SAP does not document a standard option for the invoice creator to manually add or select an ad-hoc approver when submitting the supplier invoice.

More details below:

SAP Help – Flexible Workflow for Supplier Invoices
SAP Help – Manage Workflows for Supplier Invoices
SAP Help – Workflow Scenarios and Tasks for Supplier Invoices

This is different from Purchase Order workflow where SAP explicitly documents that ad-hoc workflow steps can be created for purchase orders in approval

More details below:

SAP Help – Manage Purchase Orders
SAP Learning – Adding Ad-Hoc Approver to the Purchase Order Workflow in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Question 2.

MRM_WORKFLOW_AGENTS is a supported BAdI for custom workflow agent determination for supplier invoices. However, it is not the same as a requester-facing ad-hoc approver function. The BAdI can be used to determine approvers dynamically based on invoice data and custom logic. It does not provide a standard field, popup, or people-picker that allows the invoice creator to manually choose the approver at the time of invoice submission

A possible clean-core approach, if required by the business, would be to capture the requested approver in a supported custom field and use MRM_WORKFLOW_AGENTS to derive the workflow agent from that value. However this would be a custom agent-determination design and would need to be validated in the target app/UI. It should not be treated as standard PO-like ad-hoc approver functionality

More details below:

SAP Help Workflow Features for Supplier Invoices (Parked as Complete)

SAP Help Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) for Invoice Processing


Question 3.

I did not find a public SAP roadmap item confirming that PO-like ad-hoc approver selection is planned for native Public Cloud Edition Supplier Invoice workflows. Therefore, if this is required as standard product functionality the recommended approach is to submit an improvement request via SAP Customer Influence

More details below:

SAP KBA 2963059 – How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Tolerance Days for Payable FBZP considered for immediate payment
entry.content:
The system considers the tolerance days in any case, even if immediate net payment is necessary.

We want the tolerance days only to be considered for payments with cash discount.

Is there a way to achieve this in standard?

Regards,

Sravanthi

entry.title: Re: MTO, quantity in SO change. How should I deal with Pu...
entry.content:
Hello @Moon3 

You need to start working with MRP exceptions. In a situation like yours stock/requirements list (transactions MD04 and MD07) will report exception message 26 in group 6:

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: A Holistic Approach to SAP Security: Introducing the Secure Operations Map (SOM)
entry.content:
Hi Oxana! 

Hope everything's good with you and yours!

Thanks for this blog to help clarify the different Layers we need to consider for Security in a Project/Customer.

Much important information that every customer should consider and have/define internal procedures for them.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Change field selection in "Create Customer Project"
entry.content:
Hello @yKim ,

Thank you for your question.

The rendering of the Cost Center field as a dropdown is a standard behavior and cannot be changed via key user configuration. It can be changed to a search option similar to Project Manager only via custom development.

The dropdown only shows cost centers assigned to the selected Service Organization, not all the cost centers in the system.  The recommended approach is to review the cost center assignments in the Manage Service Organizations app and distribute them across multiple, purpose-specific Service Organizations. This is the standard way to keep the list short and relevant.

For Japanese language search issue, please ensure that the users log in with their language set to JA so the JA texts are retrieved and displayed in the dropdown. Also, please ensure that Japanese (JA) texts are maintained for all the cost centers in Manage Cost Centers app, if not the system falls back to English.

Hope this helps.

Regards,

Sreevidya

entry.title: Restricting User Access in SAP SuccessFactors via IAS & IPS : A Complete Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
What This Blog Covers: A real-world implementation where a global organisation needed to restrict specific country-based users from accessing SAP SuccessFactors during a phased go-live rollout. We cover the complete journey — architecture, IPS transformation rules, IAS group management, conditional authentication, troubleshooting 10 real issues encountered, and how to cleanly remove restrictions post go-live. All sensitive information has been masked for publication.

1. Background and Business Requirement

During a phased go-live of SAP SuccessFactors (Recruiting, Onboarding, and Benefits modules), the requirement was to temporarily restrict users from a specific country entity from accessing the instance — while allowing all other global users to continue their normal SSO login via Microsoft Azure AD.

Key requirements:

#

Requirement

1

Restrict specific users based on their company/entity assignment in SuccessFactors

2

Keep restricted users active in IAS — no account deactivation

3

Non-restricted users must continue SSO via Azure AD without any disruption

4

Restriction must be temporary and easily reversible post Go-Live

5

Support both restricting all users in an entity AND specific named users

6

Work within the existing IAS + IPS + Azure AD SSO architecture

2. Solution Architecture

The solution connects SAP SuccessFactors, IPS, IAS, and Microsoft Azure AD. SAP SuccessFactors is the master source of users. IPS provisions users and group membership into IAS. IAS controls authentication routing and access decisions.

Component

Role in Solution

SAP SuccessFactors

Master source of users. Company/entity field (custom02) identifies restricted users.

IPS Source System

Reads users from SF, applies transformation rules, tags restricted users with IAS group

IPS Target System

Writes user attributes and group membership to IAS

IAS

Identity Provider for SP-initiated SSO. Applies access restriction rules.

Microsoft Azure AD

Corporate IdP used for Employee, Partner and Contractor SSO

IAS Group

Tags restricted users — referenced by Conditional Auth and Risk Based Auth rules

3. Solution Design — Three Layer Approach

The restriction requires all three layers working together. Removing any single layer causes the restriction to fail silently.

Critical: Without the Conditional Authentication Priority 1 rule, restricted users match the existing Employee/Partner rules and are routed directly to Azure AD — completely bypassing the Risk Based Auth DENY rule. All three layers are mandatory.

4. IPS Configuration

4.1 Identify the Company Field

The company/entity field in SF is mapped as custom02 in the IPS source transformation. The exact raw value must be verified from IPS trace logs — not the display label shown in the SF UI. Enable ips.trace.skipped.entity.content = true in IPS Source System Properties to see exact raw field values in provisioning logs.

Important: Always verify that custom02 is listed in the sf.user.attributes property of the IPS Source System. If missing, the SF OData API will never return this field and all custom02 conditions will silently fail with no error message shown.

4.2 Source Transformation — Three Approaches

Option A — Restrict all users in an entity (company field):

{    "condition": "$.custom02 == 'YOUR_COMPANY_CODE_VALUE'",    "constant": "Restricted_Users_Group",    "optional": true,    "targetPath": "$.groups[0].value"}

Option B — Restrict specific User IDs only:

{    "condition": "$.userId == 'USER_ID_1' || $.userId == 'USER_ID_2' || $.userId == 'USER_ID_3'",    "constant": "Restricted_Users_Group",    "optional": true,    "targetPath": "$.groups[0].value"}

Option C — Company entity with named exclusions (Recommended for large user sets):

{    "condition": "$.custom02 == 'YOUR_COMPANY_CODE_VALUE' && $.userId != 'EXCLUDE_USER_1' && $.userId != 'EXCLUDE_USER_2'",    "constant": "Restricted_Users_Group",    "optional": true,    "targetPath": "$.groups[0].value"}

Recommendation: Option C is best when you have a large number of users to restrict but a smaller exclusion list. It automatically applies to new hires in the entity and only requires maintaining the exclusion list — much shorter and more maintainable than listing hundreds of individual user IDs.

Note on userId: The userId field in IPS maps to the SF User ID field — NOT the Username or Login Name. These are completely different values. The userId is often numeric (e.g. 1103908). Always verify in SF Admin Center → Manage Users before using in conditions.

4.3 Target Transformation — Groups Passthrough Mapping

Add the following as the last mapping in the user section of the IAS target transformation (after the passwordStatus or sendMail mappings):

{    "sourcePath": "$.groups[*].value",    "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true,    "optional": true,    "targetPath": "$.groups[?(@.value)]"} // Note: resolveEntityIds intentionally NOT used here.// Manually created IAS groups require direct passthrough.// resolveEntityIds silently drops group assignment when UUID not in IPS cache.

Do NOT use resolveEntityIds for manually created IAS groups. It silently drops the group assignment when the UUID is not in the IPS cache — no error is shown in provisioning logs.

4.4 Critical IPS Properties to Verify

Property

Required Value

Why It Matters

sf.user.attributes

Must include custom02

Without this, SF OData API never returns the field to IPS

sf.user.filter

status eq 'active'

Never filter to single user — causes mass deletion of all other IAS users

ips.trace.skipped.entity.content

true (for debugging)

Shows exact raw field values in provisioning logs

ips.trace.failed.entity.content

true (for debugging)

Shows full error details for failed entity provisioning

4.5 IPS Job Execution Sequence

Always follow this exact sequence. Skipping or reversing the order causes group assignment to fail silently with no error message.

Step

Action

Navigation

Purpose

1

Run READ job on IAS Source System

IPS → Source Systems → [IAS source system] → Jobs → Run Now → READ

Populates IPS internal UUID cache for IAS groups including the restriction group

2

Run RESYNC job on SF Source System

IPS → Source Systems → [SF source system] → Jobs → Run Now → RESYNC

Reads from SF AND writes to IAS. A READ-only job never writes to the IAS target

3

Verify Provisioning Logs

IPS → Provisioning Logs

Confirm restricted users show SUCCESS status

5. IAS Configuration

5.1 Create User Group

Navigation: IAS Admin → Users & Authorizations → User Groups → Add

Field

Value / Note

Group Name

Restricted_Users_Group

Members

Automatically populated via IPS Resync job

Naming Rule

Group name is case sensitive — must exactly match the constant value in the IPS source transformation

5.2 Conditional Authentication — Add Priority 1 Rule

Navigation: IAS Admin → Applications & Resources → Applications → [SF Application] → Authentication and Access → Conditional Authentication

Click Add Rule and configure as Priority 1 (use the up arrow to move to the top):

Priority

Identity Provider

User Group

User Type

Email Domain

IP Range

1 (NEW)

Identity Authentication

Restricted_Users_Group

Any

Any

Any

2

Corporate IdP (Azure AD)

Any

Employee

Any

Any

3

Corporate IdP (Azure AD)

Any

Partner

Any

Any

4

Corporate IdP (Azure AD)

Any

External

Any

Any

Default

Identity Authentication

—

—

—

—

Identity Provider must be Identity Authentication (IAS native) — NOT Azure AD. Setting it to Azure AD routes the user to Azure AD first which authenticates them, completely bypassing the Risk Based Auth DENY rule.

5.3 Risk Based Authentication — Add DENY Rule

Navigation: IAS Admin → Applications & Resources → Applications → [SF Application] → Authentication and Access → Risk Based Authentication

Click Create Rule and move it to Priority 1. Set Default Action = Allow:

Priority

Action

Group

IP Range

Forwarded IP

User Type

1 (NEW)

Deny

Restricted_Users_Group

Any

Any

Any

2+

Existing rules

Existing groups

Any

Any

Any

Default

Allow

—

—

—

—

6. End-to-End Flow

Restricted Country User Login Attempt

Step

Event

1

User hits SF URL (SP-initiated SSO)

2

SF redirects to IAS

3

Conditional Auth Priority 1: User Group = Restricted_Users_Group → MATCH

4

Routed to: Identity Authentication (IAS native login page)

5

Risk Based Auth Priority 1: Group = Restricted_Users_Group → DENY

6

Result: "Sorry, we could not authenticate you." — user is BLOCKED from SF, user remains ACTIVE in IAS

Non-Restricted User Login

Step

Event

1

User hits SF URL (SP-initiated SSO)

2

SF redirects to IAS

3

Conditional Auth Priority 1: Restricted_Users_Group → NO MATCH

4

Priority 2/3/4: Employee / Partner / External → Corporate IdP (Azure AD)

5

Result: Normal SSO login via Azure AD — zero impact on non-restricted users

 

User Type

Conditional Auth

Routed To

Risk Based Auth

Final Result

Restricted country user

Priority 1 matches (group)

IAS native login

DENY fires

Blocked — cannot authenticate

Non-restricted user

Priority 1 no match → P2/3/4

Azure AD (Corporate IdP)

Not evaluated

Normal SSO login success

7. Troubleshooting — Real Issues Encountered

Every issue below was encountered and resolved during the actual implementation. These are documented to help others avoid the same pitfalls.

#

Issue

Root Cause

Resolution

1

Group not populated in IAS after Resync

Groups passthrough mapping missing from target transformation user section

Add groups passthrough mapping in USER section of IAS target transformation

2

Groups mapping silently failing

resolveEntityIds cannot find UUID for manually created group

Remove resolveEntityIds from the groups mapping entirely

3

DENY rule not triggering

Conditional Auth routing user to Azure AD — DENY rule bypassed entirely

Add Priority 1 Conditional Auth rule routing restricted group to IAS native login first

4

User filter causing mass deletion in IAS

sf.user.filter set to single user — IPS flagged all other users for deletion

Always keep sf.user.filter = status eq 'active' — never filter to single user

5

READ job not updating IAS group

READ job only caches data internally — never writes to IAS target

Use RESYNC job which reads from SF AND writes to IAS target

6

userId condition not matching users

SF userId is often numeric — not the username or login name field

Verify exact userId in SF Admin Center → Manage Users before using in conditions

7

custom02 condition always fails

custom02 not listed in sf.user.attributes property — SF never returns the field

Add custom02 to sf.user.attributes in IPS Source System Properties

8

custom02 value mismatch

SF UI display name differs from the actual OData API raw value returned

Enable trace logging and check exact raw value in IPS provisioning logs

9

Password prompt instead of Access Denied

Risk Based Auth DENY evaluates only after the login attempt is submitted

Expected behaviour — users cannot authenticate regardless of password entered

10

IAS group still populated after removing IPS condition

IPS does not remove group membership when condition is removed — only adds or updates

Manually remove all members from IAS group OR delete the group entirely

8. Removing the Restriction (Post Go-Live)

Important: Removing the IPS condition does NOT automatically remove users from the IAS group. IPS only adds or updates group membership — it never deletes it. Manual removal of IAS group members is always required when lifting a restriction.

Step

Action

Details

1

Remove Group Rule from IPS Source Transformation

Delete the entire condition mapping block from the source transformation. Save the transformation.

2

Run IPS Jobs in Sequence

READ on IAS source → RESYNC on SF source. IPS will no longer re-add users to the group since the condition has been removed.

3

Manually Remove Group Members in IAS

IAS → Users & Authorizations → User Groups → Restricted_Users_Group → select all members → Remove. Alternatively, delete the group entirely if no longer needed.

4

Delete IAS Rules from All Applications

For every SF application in IAS, remove the Conditional Authentication Priority 1 rule AND the Risk Based Authentication DENY rule.

5

Verify SSO Works for Previously Restricted Users

Test 3–5 previously restricted users — they should now be redirected to Azure AD and login successfully via normal SSO.

9. Key Lessons Learned

#

Lesson

Why It Matters

1

Always run READ on IAS source BEFORE Resync

Populates IPS UUID cache needed for group resolution

2

Always use RESYNC job — not READ job

READ job only caches data internally and never writes to IAS target

3

Never filter sf.user.filter to a single user

Causes mass deletion of all other users in IAS on next Resync

4

No resolveEntityIds for manually created IAS groups

Silently drops group assignment with no error shown in logs

5

Conditional Auth Priority 1 rule is mandatory

Without it Azure AD authenticates users before DENY rule can fire

6

DENY rule belongs in Risk Based Auth — not Conditional Auth

Conditional Auth only routes users to IdPs — it cannot deny access

7

SF userId can be numeric — always verify exact value

The userId and Username fields are completely different in SF

8

custom02 must be listed in sf.user.attributes

Field never returned by SF OData API if not explicitly listed

9

IAS group name is case sensitive

Must match the IPS transformation constant value exactly

10

Removing IPS condition does not remove IAS group membership

Manual removal from IAS group is always required after lifting restriction

11

Groups mapping must be in USER section of target transformation

Group section may be set to ignore:true — placing mapping there has no effect

12

Use custom02 + exclusion list for large user sets

Shorter condition, auto-applies to new hires, much easier long-term maintenance

Summary: Restricting access in SAP SuccessFactors via IAS and IPS requires a three-layer approach — IPS transformation tags users with a restriction group, IAS Conditional Authentication routes them to IAS native login at Priority 1, and IAS Risk Based Authentication DENY rule blocks access. Users remain active in IAS throughout. The most critical insight: Conditional Authentication and Risk Based Authentication must work together — one routes the user, the other denies them. Neither works alone for this use case. When lifting the restriction, always manually remove members from the IAS group as removing the IPS condition alone does not trigger group membership deletion.

This blog documents a real implementation with real challenges encountered and resolved. Every troubleshooting point above was an actual issue from the project. I hope this helps others working on similar access restriction requirements with SAP SuccessFactors, IAS, and IPS. Feel free to leave questions in the comments!

entry.title: Join the SAP S/4HANA Cloud for product compliance 2608 Release Highlights webinar
entry.content:
Hi Product Compliance Community!

Get ready for our mid-year updates with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2608 Release Highlights!
Join our Early Release webinar this July to explore the latest updates and enhancements live.

SAP S/4HANA for product compliance Session:

🕐 Time: 1:15 pm CET
🌐 Location: Online
⏳ Duration: 30 minutes

entry.title: Update on SAP Fioneer Extensions for FS-CD and ICM
entry.content:
Innovations for Insurance Industry from SAP Fioneer

Overview about Fioneer Commissions and Payments Extensions 

Fioneer Commissions and Payments Extensions (FCPE) support integration with external systems to execute business processes such as broker settlement or real-time payments. Furthermore, it supports a "separation of concerns," meaning that, for example, a business process running in an upstream/external system doesn't have to worry about which dependent processes need to be started in a downstream system. It acts as an integration component to streamline common integration challenges to process broker settlements, outgoing instant payments, or business transactions from insurance core solutions.

The suite is available as Fioneer Payments Extensions for FS-CD customers or as Fioneer Commissions Extensions for ICM customers. It bundles current and future innovations from SAP Fioneer for insurance industry such as

  • Settlement Hub
  • Real-Time Payment
  • Business Process Integration

Settlement Hub

Settlement Hub is a feature for SAP Collections and Disbursements (FS-CD) and provides a holistic solution to manage all relevant aspects of broker collections. 

Fiori App Settlement Hub

It offers a web service that is compatible with standards like BiPRO and a Fiori application that allows users to manage all settlements and clarifications. Furthermore it enables the integration of an ISML model to reconcial policy data, settled premiums, commissions etc. with open items in FS-CD.

Real-Time Payment

With Real-Time Payment, you can manage outgoing payments in real time. 

You can view the real-time payments in the Fiori App Instant Payments. The app allows you to select open outgoing payments and to release them for real-time payment. You can change the payment method, the recipient and the bank account before you release the payment. For example, you can pay a commission to a broker.

Instant Payment App

The underlying services are also integrated into FS-CM for instant claim payments.

Business Process Integration

Business Process Integration (BPI) is an feature that is used for the functional integration and harmonization of processes. It supports a “separation of concerns”, whereby a main business process that is executed in an upstream system doesn’t need to care about which dependent processes need to be started in a downstream system.

Architecture BPI

 

It relies on the usage of Integration Suite to connect with multiple insurance core systems.

Further Information

  • Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISML)
  • SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP )

entry.title: Unknown administrators
entry.content:
Hi SAP Support,

We are trying to find the Administrators for a company in SAP for Me. This is for S-user ID expiry and other requirements. We have some S-User ID expiry extension requests , but there is no information on the current admins.
Kindy help with this information.

Regards,



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unknown administrators
entry.content:
Hi,

You or your colleagues in the given organization can find the admins in SAP for Me --> My Important Contacts: https://me.sap.com/userscontacts/impcont

They can then extend the S-user expiry as per this KBA:
2928052 - "S-user ID Lifetime information - SAP for Me"

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: error message HTTP Open failed: HTTP Internal Error i...
entry.content: To resolve this error, please follow steps outlined in KBA 3562546 - Gateway client shows HTTP Open failed: HTTP Internal Error - SAP for Me
